preconditions.text: The conference should support CDC's mission and further CDC's mission of promoting health and quality of life by preventing and controlling disease, injury, and disability.
Scientific meetings sponsored by CDC must meet the Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences.
Requests for CDC sponsorship of conferences that include funding must be approved by the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) or his/her designee, and processed through financial support procedures.
Requests for CDC sponsorship of conferences that do not include funding must be approved by the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) or his/her designee.
Sponsorship must be consistent with CDC's policy on Guidance for Collaboration with the Private Sector and the HHS Office of the General Counsel (OGC), Ethics Division Co-Sponsorship Guidance.
Any conference sponsored by CDC that is held in domestic facilities must be fully accessible to the public as required by the Americans with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

The conference organizer(s) may use CDC's name only in factual publicity for the conference and should understand that CDC involvement in the conference does not necessarily indicate support for, or endorsement of, the organizer's general policies, activities, or products.

steps.text: Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.

Establish a written process for approving conference funding which includes information on how proposals for conference funding will be solicited, elements required in a proposal, a process for review and approval of proposals by the CC/CO/NC.
Ensure review by a Center or Division level appointed committee composed of 3 staff members or peers per meeting.
Final approval is made by the CC/CO/NC Director.
Document review and approval of conference funding by the CC/CO/NC including a copy of the proposal, how the review was conducted, criteria for review, and whether funding was approved.
Ensure a grant, cooperative agreement, interagency agreement, contract, or purchase order is in place when CDC is asked to sponsor a conference with a non-federal organization and funds are provided.
Obtain a co-sponsorship agreement whenever CDC is asked to sponsor a conference with a non-federal organization.
Provide written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request if the CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC does not wish to sponsor the conference.
Provide written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval if the CC/CO/NC is interested in sponsoring the conference.
Include specific language regarding CDC's active participation and review requirements in the tentative approval response.
Review the full final conference agenda and other conference information.
Provide written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor after final approval is provided.

constraints.text: No verbal disapproval of sponsorship may be provided.
No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).

steps.text: Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG prior to approving any request or issuing any obligation documents.
Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility stating that facilities, goods and services are fully ADA compliant.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist, and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
